questions.q: What was the real birth name of the legendary actor Dilip Kumar?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar was born Mohammed Yusuf Khan in Peshawar in 1922 and adopted his screen name early in his career.

questions.q: By what popular epithet was the Hindi film actor Dilip Kumar widely known?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar earned the title 'Tragedy King' for his intense, sorrowful roles in films like Devdas and Andaz.

questions.q: In which 1960 historical epic did Dilip Kumar play Prince Salim opposite Madhubala's Anarkali?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar played Prince Salim in K. Asif's monumental 1960 epic Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: In which year was the actor Dilip Kumar born?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar was born on 11 December 1922 in Peshawar, in present-day Pakistan.

questions.q: In which 1955 Bimal Roy film did Dilip Kumar play the doomed, alcoholic lover of literary fame?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar played the title role in Bimal Roy's 1955 adaptation of Sharat Chandra Chattopadhyay's Devdas.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar made his film debut in which 1944 Bombay Talkies production?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar made his acting debut in the 1944 Bombay Talkies film Jwar Bhata.

questions.q: In which city, then part of British India, was Dilip Kumar born?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar was born in Peshawar, which is now in Pakistan.

questions.q: Which actress did Dilip Kumar marry in 1966 and remain married to until his death?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar married actress Saira Banu in 1966, and they remained married until his death in 2021.

questions.q: In which year did the veteran actor Dilip Kumar pass away?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar died on 7 July 2021 in Mumbai at the age of 98.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar's screen acting was known for which distinctive quality?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar is credited with bringing a naturalistic, restrained and internalised acting style to Hindi cinema.

questions.q: In which 1949 Mehboob Khan film did Dilip Kumar star alongside Raj Kapoor and Nargis in a love triangle?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar starred with Raj Kapoor and Nargis in Mehboob Khan's 1949 hit Andaz.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar shared screen space with Amitabh Bachchan in which acclaimed 1982 Ramesh Sippy film?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar played a principled police officer opposite Amitabh Bachchan in Ramesh Sippy's 1982 film Shakti.

questions.q: Which 1957 B. R. Chopra film paired Dilip Kumar with Vyjayanthimala in a tale of a tonga driver resisting mechanisation?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar starred opposite Vyjayanthimala in B. R. Chopra's 1957 film Naya Daur.

questions.q: Which 1961 film, depicting two brothers on opposite sides of the law, did Dilip Kumar produce and which was directed by Nitin Bose?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar produced and starred in the 1961 dacoit drama Gunga Jumna, with his brother Nasir Khan playing the second lead.

questions.q: In Bimal Roy's 1958 reincarnation drama Madhumati, Dilip Kumar played the male lead opposite which actress in the title role?
questions.explanation: Vyjayanthimala played the title role in Bimal Roy's 1958 film Madhumati, opposite Dilip Kumar.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar won the first-ever Filmfare Award for Best Actor, presented in 1954, for his performance in which film?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar won the inaugural Filmfare Best Actor Award, presented in 1954, for the film Daag (1952).

questions.q: Which civilian honour was conferred on Dilip Kumar by the Government of India in 1991?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 1991 and later the Padma Vibhushan in 2015.

questions.q: Which prestigious lifetime achievement honour of Indian cinema did Dilip Kumar receive in 1994?
options: National Film Award for Best Actor
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour, in 1994.

questions.q: After a long break from the screen, Dilip Kumar returned in 1981 in which Manoj Kumar multi-starrer?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar made his comeback after a hiatus with Manoj Kumar's 1981 multi-starrer Kranti.

questions.q: In the 1955 film Azaad, Dilip Kumar took on a rare role of what kind, a departure from his tragic image?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar played a swashbuckling, comic hero in the 1955 film Azaad, breaking from his 'Tragedy King' mould.

questions.q: Which statement best describes the colour format of Mughal-e-Azam (1960), in which Dilip Kumar starred as Prince Salim?
options: It was shot entirely in colour
It was largely in black-and-white with a few colour sequences
It was shot entirely in sepia tone
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam (1960) was mostly black-and-white with a few colour sequences, and was later fully colourised in 2004.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar holds a record for winning the Filmfare Best Actor Award how many times?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar won the Filmfare Best Actor Award eight times, a record he held for decades.

questions.q: In 1998, the Government of Pakistan conferred which of its highest civilian honours on Dilip Kumar?
questions.explanation: Pakistan awarded Dilip Kumar the Nishan-e-Imtiaz, its highest civilian award, in 1998.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar's final film appearance in a leading role came in which 1998 release?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar's last film appearance in a lead role was in the 1998 film Qila.

questions.q: In Subhash Ghai's 1991 film Saudagar, Dilip Kumar shared the screen in a feud with which other veteran star, reuniting them after decades?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar and Raaj Kumar starred as feuding patriarchs in Subhash Ghai's 1991 film Saudagar.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar reportedly turned down a role in David Lean's 1962 epic, a part that was instead played by Omar Sharif. Which film was it?
options: The Bridge on the River Kwai
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar reportedly declined the role of Sherif Ali in Lawrence of Arabia (1962), which went to Omar Sharif.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar was nominated to which house of the Indian Parliament in 2000?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar was a nominated member of the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of Parliament, from 2000 to 2006.

questions.q: In the 1967 film Ram Aur Shyam, Dilip Kumar famously played a double role as twins separated at birth. Which two actresses played the leading ladies in that film?
questions.explanation: In Ram Aur Shyam (1967), Dilip Kumar played twin roles, with Waheeda Rehman and Mumtaz as the leading ladies.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar played a blind landlord seeking revenge in which 1966 film, an adaptation of Thomas Hardy's 'The Mayor of Casterbridge'?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar starred in Dil Diya Dard Liya (1966), a Hindi adaptation loosely based on Thomas Hardy's novel 'The Mayor of Casterbridge'.

questions.q: Dilip Kumar, Raj Kapoor and Dev Anand are collectively remembered as which trio that dominated Hindi cinema of the 1950s and 1960s?
options: The Golden Triumvirate of Hindi cinema
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar, Raj Kapoor and Dev Anand are remembered as the leading male triumvirate of Hindi cinema's golden age.
